{"credits": [{"type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/97803816?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/mauriceng98/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/mauriceng98/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/mauriceng98/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/mauriceng98/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/mauriceng98", "id": 97803816, "login": "mauriceng98", "node_id": "U_kgDOBdReKA", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/mauriceng98/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/mauriceng98/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/mauriceng98/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/mauriceng98/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/mauriceng98/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/mauriceng98", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-48804", "cvss": {"score": 7.5, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 7.5, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-770", "name": "Allocation of Resources Without Limits or Throttling"}], "description": "### Impact
The python-socketio server stores binary `EVENT` and `ACK` messages in memory while it waits to receive their binary attachments. Once all the attachments are received, these messages are then processed. An attacker can submit a binary message and intentionally omit sending one or more of its attachments to cause the message along with the partial list of received attachments to stay in memory for a long time.

### Patches
Version 5.16.2 takes the following measures to address this issue:
- Binary packets are only accepted from authenticated clients.
- When a client disconnects, the server checks if there is a partial binary message being held for the client and deletes it.", "ghsa_id": "GHSA-5w7q-77mv-v69f", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-06-26T20:51:58Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-5w7q-77mv-v69f", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-5w7q-77mv-v69f"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-48804"}], "nvd_published_at": null, "published_at": "2026-06-26T20:51:58Z", "references": ["https://github.com/miguelgrinberg/python-socketio/security/advisories/GHSA-5w7q-77mv-v69f", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-5w7q-77mv-v69f"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/miguelgrinberg/python-socketio/security-advisories/GHSA-5w7q-77mv-v69f", "severity": "high", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/miguelgrinberg/python-socketio", "summary": "python-socketio: Binary attachment accumulation can cause denial of service", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-06-26T20:51:58Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-5w7q-77mv-v69f", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "5.16.2", "package": {"ecosystem": "pip", "name": "python-socketio"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "<= 5.16.1"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

reasons: 資産不一致または判定情報不足

title: python-socketio: Binary attachment accumulation can cause denial of service
